chunks.text: What's up guys, you are in the 11th video of the Sigma Web Development course.  We have completed 10 videos.  So one like is enough for the consistency of all of us.  In today's video, we will see about Semantic Tags.  Because they are very important for SEO.

chunks.text: And to understand the structure of the overall website,  search engines are very happy to see this structure.  That means overall SEO of your website is going to be very good if you use these tags.  What are these tags? Why are these tags?  How to use them?

By putting an H1, I am going to set this image and preview it here.  So this is the image.  To explain a little, I will tell you what are Semantic Tags?

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: Semantic Tags are for browsers and web crawlers  who want to understand your website properly.  They tell them what is the content of your website?  And what is the purpose of which place and which tag?  If you don't use Semantic Tags in HTML,

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: then your work will be done.  It's not like if you don't use Semantic Tags,  then your website won't look good.  You can do a good design.  But we want to explain to Google and Bing search engines about our website.

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: And if we explain them about our website,  then they will be able to understand our website.  And they will rank our website on Google, Bing, respective search engines.  And we want the crawlers to understand our website.  And we want whoever wants to understand our website to understand it well.

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: That's why there are some special tags.  Like header, nav, main, article, figure.  All these are special tags in HTML  which help in structuring your website.  Now if there is something in the header, then I know that it is the header.

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: That is, it is at the top.  If there is something in the footer, then I will understand that  the website has started ending from here.  This footer has started here.  This footer could also be div.

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: But no, if I am using Semantic HTML,  then I will use the footer tag.  Along with that, where I was making my navigation bar in the span tag,  this time I will not make my navigation bar in the span tag.  I will make my navigation bar in the nav tag.

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: So these kinds of differences explain our search engines very well  about what our website is about.  So I will show you some examples of semantic tags here.  And I hope you understood what semantic tags are.  Now see, there are download folders like our computer.

chunks.text: Many of you must have a download folder  that you have got all the files of the world in it.  90% of the files of your computer are in the download folder of many people.  But we should not do this.  Some people go to such an extent that they start spreading files

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: in their local disk C, in the windows folder.  And some people keep files in system 32 too, friends.  So if you spread your files in your entire computer,  then definitely it is not arranged in one way.  But if you arrange it in folders,

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: that you have this file in one folder.  If you want to put a secret file in a folder,  you put secret files.  You can make a folder called do not open disk.  If you can put some of your files in it,

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: then it will be arranged in one way.  You have arranged images in one way,  you have arranged videos in one way,  and you have arranged letters in one way,  and you have arranged e-books in one way.

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: So whenever you go to find it, you will get it.  So it will be much better for you to understand  that folder structure instead of  if you are spreading your files throughout the computer.  You definitely don't want to spread your files throughout the computer.

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: So I hope you understood why we use semantic tags.  It is very good for our SEO.  So SEO is a word, I have not used it yet.  But SEO is a very important part of search engine optimization.  Using semantic tags.

chunks.text: So what happens?  Basically, I make your website the way you make it.  You just use some special tags.  For example, let's say I am making this website.  What I will do now is I will comment this image here.

chunks.text: Now if I want a footer, let's say I want to make a footer.  Here my main content will come after the header.  So I will use the main tag.  And after that, my footer, let's say I write in the footer.  Copyright, CWH, and here I write all rights reserved.

chunks.text: Okay, I can write like this.  So here, look, copyright, all rights reserved, I wrote all this here.  Did I, uh, this, yes.  So what I will do is I will cut this.  I will put this in the main.

So we understood about the header tag that it is used to represent the top section of a web page.  Which is the header.  Then after that, we put nav, which is the navigation menu on our page, in the nav tag.

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: Ideally, the article indicates a self-containing piece of content such as a blog post or news article.  If you put a particular self-contained piece of content, then put it in the article tag.  Section represents a thematic grouping.  That is, suppose one section is talking about one thing on your page.  Then the second section will talk about something else.

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: Aside, aside means if anything is lying on the side, then it is used as an aside tag.  So here you see, typically used for sidebars or content that is tangentially related to the main content.  Like what you can do on the aside tag.  That you have to put a side note, you have to put about the author.  You must have seen a lot of blogs in which the side is also written about the author.

chunks.text: So you can put such things in the aside tag.  Then after this we have the footer tag.  I told you about this.  If you want to put copyright information or if you want to put some important links of the website, then you can put it in the footer.  Then after this, figure and fig caption.

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: What is figure and fig caption?  Whenever you want to use images, diagrams, charts, etc., then you can use figure and fig caption.  The main content area inside the main, like the main content area of ​​our page was this.  We put it in the main tag.  Then there is also a time tag.

chunks.text: Used to represent time-related information.  So you can use the time tag.  You can put date, time or anything in it.  So I hope you understand this thing now.  So these are used to enhance SEO.

chunks.title: Semantic Tags  in HTML
chunks.text: Accessibility is used to improve.  And if browsers want to make a decision about any of our pages.  For example, a browser has a new feature that it will read our content aloud.  Like Edge has a feature.  So the browser can decide that I am only reading the main content aloud.

chunks.text: Now think about it, if someone wants to read this page aloud, then how will he read it aloud?  Code with Harry, Home, Courses, Tutorial.  You don't want to be allowed to read like this.  You want the main content to be read.  HTML, Semantic Tags.

And the most important reason to introduce semantic tags in HTML5 is to arrange things.  So that the browser or whoever is parsing the content.
